name: Caminito del Rey from El Chorro
name_original: Caminito del Rey desde El Chorro
region_intro: Hiking trails in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes are defined by a dramatic gorge carved by the Guadalhorce River, featuring towering limestone walls that reach hundreds of meters in height. The landscape is characterized by its unique geological formations, including Jurassic limestones and karst relief. This natural park offers a diverse terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes The most popular hiking route is Caminito del Rey from El Chorro, a 5.4 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers suspended walkways and panoramic views of the gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gaitanejo Trail, a difficult 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This trail provides scenic views within the wider gorge area, typically completed in 1 hour 15 minutes. Local hikers also love the Caminito del Rey – Caminito del Rey loop from Puerto de las Atalayas, a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain and offering extensive views of the gorge, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Hiking in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes is defined by dramatic gorges, towering limestone walls, and the Guadalhorce River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1500 reviews. More than 15000 hikers have used komoot to explore Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes offers a wide range of hiking experiences, with over 45 routes recorded on komoot. These include 10 easy, 32 moderate, and 6 difficult trails, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: The mild weather and lush vegetation in spring and autumn make these seasons ideal for visiting Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes. The dramatic gorge and its unique geological formations are particularly stunning during these times.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: While the famous Caminito del Rey has age restrictions, there are other trails in the wider Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes area that are suitable for families. Look for routes marked as 'easy' or 'moderate' on komoot, which often feature less challenging terrain and shorter distances. The park's diverse landscape offers plenty to explore for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: Dogs are generally not permitted on the Caminito del Rey trail itself due to safety and conservation regulations. For other trails within the broader Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes natural park, it's advisable to check specific trail rules or local signage, as regulations can vary. Always keep dogs on a leash and ensure they do not disturb wildlife.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on hikes in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: Hikes in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes are characterized by dramatic gorges, towering limestone walls, and the Guadalhorce River. You can expect varied terrain, from suspended walkways on the Caminito del Rey to paths through pine forests and karst landscapes. Elevation changes can be significant, especially on more difficult routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995633003/caminito-del-rey-from-el-chorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey from El Chorro&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 600 meters of ascent and descent.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/431048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey – Caminito del Rey loop from Puerto de las Atalayas&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer, comprehensive experience of the gorge and surrounding areas. Other trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17224850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mountains – Cave Houses of Sierra Parda loop from Parking Ardales&lt;/a&gt; also provide circular options with diverse scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in the area?
A: Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes is rich in natural wonders. Beyond the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, you can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Gorge&lt;/a&gt; itself, with its towering limestone walls. Other notable features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1308367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcon de cristal&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge over the Gaitanes Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and fascinating geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6599193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Túnel de los Tafonis&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6276908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave Houses of Sierra Parda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I park when visiting Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes for a hike?
A: Parking is available at various access points to the natural park. For routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17224850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mountains – Cave Houses of Sierra Parda loop&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38294477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Convento loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can find parking near Ardales. For the Caminito del Rey, specific parking areas are designated near the northern and southern entrances, often requiring a shuttle bus to connect the start and end points.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking areas in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: Yes, public transport options are available, particularly for accessing the Caminito del Rey. There are train services to El Chorro, which is a common starting or ending point for the Caminito. Additionally, shuttle buses operate between the northern and southern access points of the Caminito del Rey to facilitate the linear walk. It's recommended to check current schedules and routes in advance.

Q: Do I need a permit to hike in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes, especially for the Caminito del Rey?
A: Yes, a permit (ticket) is required to walk the Caminito del Rey. It is highly recommended to book your tickets well in advance, as access is controlled to ensure safety and preserve the natural environment. For other trails within the natural park, permits are generally not required, but always respect local regulations and signage.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes is a biodiversity hotspot. You might spot various birds of prey, including griffon vultures, golden eagles, and peregrine falcons, making it excellent for birdwatching. Mammals such as mountain goats inhabit higher areas, while otters, foxes, and wild boar can be found throughout the park. Keep an eye out for the protected cliff-dwelling orchid, Rupicapnos Africana, in spring.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Desfiladero De Los Gaitanes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1500 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic and unique landscapes, the thrill of the Caminito del Rey, and the stunning panoramic views of the gorge. The diverse geological formations and rich biodiversity also contribute to a memorable outdoor experience.
tips: Impressive bridge at the end of the gorge.
General admission: 10 euros. Includes access and free visit. Guided tour of the Caminito del Rey: 18 euros. Includes access and visit with a tour guide in a group of up to 20 people.
The view from the bridge shows on the one hand the route taken and on the other the Guadalhorce river.
The most beautiful moment when you walk over the bridge. Enjoy it
